title: Text Line Sorter
description: Sort lines of text alphabetically, by length, or randomly. Remove duplicate lines and empty lines in one click. Free online tool.
placeholder: Enter or paste text — one item per line...

howToUse.label: Enter your text
howToUse.description: Paste or type your text into the left textarea — one item per line. Works great for name lists, URL collections, or word lists.

howToUse.label: Choose a sort mode
howToUse.description: Select from A→Z, Z→A, length ascending, length descending, or random shuffle.

howToUse.label: Apply options and copy
howToUse.description: Toggle case-sensitivity, duplicate removal, and empty-line removal. Copy the result from the right panel.

faq.question: How do I sort a list alphabetically?
faq.answer: Paste your list (one item per line) into the input and click 'A → Z'. The result appears instantly in the right panel. Click 'Z → A' for reverse alphabetical order.

faq.question: Can I remove duplicate lines?
faq.answer: Yes. Check 'Remove duplicates' to keep only the first occurrence of each line. With 'Case-sensitive' off, 'Apple', 'apple', and 'APPLE' are treated as the same value.

faq.question: What can I use this for?
faq.answer: Common uses: sorting name lists, deduplicating URL collections, organizing code variable lists, and cleaning up glossaries. The 'Remove empty lines' option removes extra blank lines that sneak in from copy-pasting.

faq.question: Is my text sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. All sorting happens entirely in your browser. Your text is never sent to any external server.
